campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investment accounts performed and overall spending. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to connect different types of transit like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment account performance and transactions
City budget and spending report for July
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and pedestrian access

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance and skipped the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council then scheduled this ordinance to be voted on and approved at the next regular meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input
New ordinance introduced with expedited process
Fast-tracked to next meeting for final vote
Skipped standard first reading requirement

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several sessions in March and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting covered routine administrative approvals from previous gatherings.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from multiple March meetings (special sessions, closed sessions, and regular town council meetings)
Authorized Town Manager to execute construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Administrative record-keeping and contractor approvals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 8, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a routine meeting to organize upcoming agendas for April 14 and April 21, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to the meeting schedules, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked that all required public notices were properly filed.
keyTopics: Final agenda review for April 14 council meeting
Draft agenda planning for April 21 council meeting
Setting specific times for agenda items to manage meeting flow
Public records review for late March and early April
Compliance with public notice requirements

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate both in-person and online via Zoom. Residents who wanted to speak during the meeting could join virtually and use the 'raise hand' feature to request time to comment on specific agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Hybrid meeting format combining in-person and online attendance

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. These special recognitions were the main focus of the evening's agenda.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Pledge of Allegiance ceremony
Community heritage and inclusion initiatives

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss three main presentations: a Budget and Bites program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from March and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs for young people
March 11, 2026 meeting minutes approval
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format that allows residents to attend in person or join remotely. The city is providing multiple ways for the public to participate and submit comments, including via Zoom video conference and phone.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote options)
Zoom participation details and phone dial-in access
Written public comment submission process via eComments
City Council agenda access through Legistar system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to handle routine business and discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030. They also reviewed new project requests from individual council members and considered changing the rules for filling vacant council seats.
keyTopics: Updated federal funding plan for housing and community development programs
New project ideas submitted by council members
Changes to how the city fills empty council positions
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve gas utility financial plans and rate changes for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved reviewing the city's gas utility budget forecast and adjusting the rates that residents and businesses pay for natural gas service across three customer categories.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates for residential customers (G-1 schedule)
Gas rates for apartment buildings and commercial properties (G-2 schedule)
Gas rates for large commercial users (G-3 schedule)
Reserve fund transfers and general fund contributions for gas utilities
Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility financial forecast
